seoTitle: Edmonton vs Edward River — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Edmonton and Edward River. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Edmonton vs Edward River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Edmonton and Edward River.
heading: Edmonton vs Edward River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Edmonton, Queensland and Edward River, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Edmonton has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Edward River (30/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Edmonton or Edward River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Edmonton has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Edward River scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Edmonton scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Edward River scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Edmonton or Edward River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Edmonton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
